meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A professional case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your MVP launch.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop building features and start building revenue models. By integrating dynamic pricing logic directly into your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code, you force business validation early. This transition moves you from a passive coder to a founder shipping a profitable, scalable SaaS product.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in the abyss of endless tutorials, stacking frameworks like Jenga blocks. Your repository is a graveyard of half-baked features, and your bank account remains untouched. The silent killer of SaaS founders isn't a lack of coding skills; it's the lack of a commercial feedback loop. You aren't building a product; you're building a digital hobby.

Today, we pivot. We are going to treat your code as a revenue-generating asset rather than a collection of abstractions. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, we will embed a robust pricing engine into your MVP. It is time to stop building for the sake of the commit and start building for the sake of the customer.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the core unit of value your SaaS provides. Before writing code, you must map your pricing to your user's ROI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS MVP core features and suggest three tiered pricing models based on usage-based metrics and flat-rate subscription hybrids.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze project architecture and suggest a database schema for multi-tenant subscription tracking'

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Engine
steps.description: Build a modular pricing controller that decouples business logic from your UI to allow for A/B testing price points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable pricing controller class in TypeScript that handles plan selection, trial periods, and feature flagging based on user subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the pricing controller and integrate it with the existing auth middleware'

steps.title: Stripe Integration Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Claude Code to establish a secure, production-ready connection to Stripe without reinventing the wheel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Stripe Webhook handler that updates the local database subscription state when a payment event is received.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up stripe-node integration and create the webhook endpoint for subscription lifecycle events'

steps.title: Dynamic Feature Gating
steps.description: Implement middleware that restricts access to premium features based on the pricing tier stored in your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a reusable decorator or middleware function that checks user subscription status against required feature permissions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply subscription middleware to all premium API routes and protected components'

steps.title: Revenue Validation Loop
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and trigger a mock purchase to verify the end-to-end flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that simulates user subscription upgrades and verifies that feature access changes instantly upon payment success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the pricing flow and execute them to verify the payment-to-access lifecycle'

pro_tips: Never hardcode your pricing tiers; store them in a JSON config or database table to allow for instant adjustments without a deployment.
Always implement a 'Free Tier' that provides just enough value to hook users, but enough friction to drive them to the entry-level paid tier.
Use usage-based pricing for your most compute-heavy features to protect your margins as your user base scales.
If you aren't collecting credit card info at the start of your trial, you aren't testing demand; you are testing free-loaders.
Use Claude Code to automate the writing of your documentation so you don't lose time explaining your own pricing architecture to future hires.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a Vercel or Railway instance, set up a landing page with a waitlist, and start driving traffic to validate your pricing model against real human behavior.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By integrating your pricing engine today, you transition from a coder to a founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
